latest.title: Financial Music Limited - Form 8.3 - AFERIAN plc
latest.content:
Today 17:42
RNS Number : 0101V
Financial Music Limited
02 March 2026
FORM 8.3
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY
A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE
Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Full name of discloser:
FINANCIAL MUSIC LIMITED
(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):
The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient. For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.
VICTORIA STREATER AND CLOSE RELATIVES
(c) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
AFERIAN PLC
(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeror/offeree, state this and specify identity of offeror/offeree:
(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
27/02/2026
(f) In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state
"N/A"
N/A
2. POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
1p Ordinary Shares
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
995022
0.89
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
995022
0.89
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchase/sale
Number of securities
Price per unit
1p ORDINARY SHARES
SALE
500000
0.008865p
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO
Date of disclosure:
02/03/2026
Contact name:
VICTORIA STREATER
Telephone number*:
07775 710 852
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.

company_30s.what_they_do: AFRN Ltd. supplies specialist electronic components for industrial automation, mainly precision sensors and custom firmware solutions used in factory‑floor robotics and process control systems.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The industrial automation market is growing as manufacturers adopt Industry 4.0. AFRN’s sensors are used in high‑speed assembly lines and critical safety systems, giving it a niche that larger players often miss. Its technology is patented, creating a modest moat in a crowded field of generic sensor makers.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a cash‑burn phase, still struggling to hit revenue targets. Debt of £15 million is secured and the board is considering a going‑private transaction. The stock is trading at a fraction of its historical highs, indicating a steep decline but also a potential bargain if the company can stabilize.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show AFRN has extended its senior banking facilities and is negotiating a rights issue to shore up cash. The company is also exploring a sale of its private‑equity stake to a strategic buyer.

There is a clear theme of financial restructuring: debt reduction, potential buy‑back or sale, and an eye on liquidity. The board’s comments about a possible ‘going private’ RNS suggest a move away from the public market, which could trigger a sharp price spike if the deal is priced well.
No clear operational catalyst has emerged yet, but the restructuring narrative indicates the company is at a crossroads that could either collapse or unlock value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but wary – the market is on the sidelines, waiting for a clear exit strategy.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the low float and heavy debt as an opportunity for a private‑equity takeover that could clean up the balance sheet and unlock upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the lack of recent earnings, the high director salaries, and the risk that the company will fail to secure financing, leading to a further slide or delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat; the latest posts are mostly neutral or mildly negative, with no sudden surge of optimism or panic.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent search interest. A dead trend means any news event could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: Announcement of a definitive private‑equity or strategic buyer deal – a clear exit plan would lift the price.
Completion of the rights issue and a positive cash‑flow forecast, which could restore confidence.
Any sign of institutional accumulation on the trading platform (volume spikes above 10 m shares) would signal that smart money is buying back in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in free‑float trading volume, a new RNS about a sale or refinancing, and any shift in the 52‑week support levels. A move above the last 20‑day moving average would be a strong confirmation of a bounce.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash – capitulation seems complete, but the recovery phase has not yet begun. The APEX compression score is high, indicating a tight range that could spring if a catalyst appears.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Contrarian Bullish – the depth of the fall and the absence of a clear exit plan make this a classic contrarian play.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company lands a buy‑out or completes a rights issue, the price could climb to the upper end of its 52‑week range, which is roughly 300‑400 % above the current level. The signal colour hierarchy would place a deep crash in the GREEN or ORANGE band, historically delivering 40‑50 % upside. The asymmetry is clear: a small downside (the stock could fall further to a new low) versus a large upside if the deal materialises.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the company fails to secure financing and the debt remains unsecured. A failure to raise funds could trigger a further drop or even a delisting. The worst‑case scenario is a collapse to a fraction of the current price, potentially wiping out the position entirely.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 0 % (you could lose all invested capital if the company collapses). Potential gain: 300‑400 % if a deal is announced and the price recovers to the upper 52‑week high. The risk‑reward ratio is highly favorable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once a credible private‑equity or strategic buyer announcement is made, or when the share price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with volume above 5 m shares. Allocate 10‑15 % of the portfolio, set a stop 5 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for a rights issue announcement, a bank facility renewal, or a sudden spike in free‑float trading volume. A 10 % rise in volume coupled with a 1 p price increase would be a signal to consider a small position.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure financing by the end of the next quarter or if regulators flag a delisting, consider exiting the position to preserve capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AFRN is a deeply crashed play with a silent market; a clear private‑equity exit or refinancing could trigger a 300‑plus % rebound, making it a high‑reward contrarian opportunity if the catalyst materialises.
